text:
Person1: Hello?
Person2: Hey. What are you doing? How are you?
Person1: I'm fine. Who is this?
Person2: Georgia.
Person1: Hi....what....what's up....?
Person2: It's ok that I'm calling, right, I mean? It's ok.
Person1: Yeah. No. It's ok. What's goin' on?
Person2: I just wanted to call and talk to you, thank you for last night, try and get you before you went to work and say, "hey."
Person1: I'm going to work.
Person2: Uhhh...I am sooo tired...I stayed up too late last night, what about you, when did you go to sleep?
Person1: Not very late.
Person2: You're going to work now?
Person1: Yes.
Person2: Can I ask you a question?
Person1: Uh-huh.
Person2: Remember last night I was talking to you and I was telling you about my apartment, my rent -- ?  Do you remember?
Person1: Yes.
Person2: This is really weird and really embarrassing for me but....uh....  I was wondering if you could help me out with a little bit of money.
Person1: Me?
Person2: Yeah.
Person1: I can't really. Yeah, no. I mean. I can't afford it.
Person2: You don't even know how much it is.
Person1: I know but I....how much is it?
Person2: Like seven-fifty. Seven hundred fifty?
Person1: Yes, no, yes. I can't. I can't afford that. I'm sorry. Sorry.
Person2: Really? Please?
Person1: You have trouble, financial trouble?
Person2: Yeah. It's so hard these days and I really need it.
Person1: Yes I can't....I don't make enough money to be able to do that.
Person2: I thought you had your own business. You said you were gonna diversify and all that stuff....

Person1: What is that?
Person2: Microrecorder for Kendall -- didn't have time to wire his room. Now tell me why you joined the army or I'll jab this pen through your neck.

Person1: But, Leon, I am only here for a few days.
Person2: If you don't stay with me, I'll have to continue my fight. I'll travel wherever Russian commissions are. I'll turn them all into Buljanoffs, Iranoffs, and Kopalskis. The world will be crowded with Russian restaurants. I'll depopulate Russia. Once you saved your country by going back. This time you can save it by staying here.
Person1: Well, when it is a choice between my personal interest and the good of my country, how can I waver? No one shall say Ninotchka was a bad Russian.

Person1: Grab a seat there, boy. Want some dinner? Grab yourself an egg roll. We got everything here from a diddle-eyed-Joe to a damned-if-I-know.
Person2: No thanks.
Person1: No thanks? What does that mean? Means you ate before you came down here? All full. Is that it? Naw, I don't think so. I think you're too scared to be eatin'. Now, see we're sittin' down here, ready to negotiate, and you've already given up your shit. I'm still a mystery to you. But I know exactly where your ass is comin' from. See, if I asked you if you wanted some dinner and you grabbed an egg roll and started to chow down, I'd say to myself, "This person's acting like he hasn't got a care in the world. Who knows? Maybe he doesn't. Maybe this person's so confident, he doesn't have to worry about anything, he just sits down, eats my Chinese food, watches my TV." See? You haven't even sat down yet. On that TV there, since you've been in the room, is a woman in a revealing outfit, and you haven't even glanced. You've just been staring at me. Now, I know I'm interesting, but I'm not as interesting as that TV show.

Person1: Is there something the matter with it?
Person2: Oh! No! No... of course not... it's just I hadn't expected it to be quite so big.
Person1: Well, it's not called the Horn Resounding for nothing.  You DO know how to play the horn, don't you?
Person2: Yes... oh, yes...
Person1: Then I expect you'll be leaving first thing in the morning.

Person1: If they harass you, I, too, will resign.  And I will tell the world why.
Person2: Don't be stupid.  The world needs you, Henry; you always saw the big picture. You were my equal in many ways.  You're the only friend I've got, Henry.
Person1: You have many friends ... and admirers ...
Person2: Do you ever pray?  You know ... believe in a Supreme Being?
Person1: Uh ... not really.  You mean on my knees?
Person2: Yes.  My mother used to pray ... a lot.  It's been a long time since I really prayed.  Let's pray, Henry; let's pray a little.

Person1: Lasher touched you and you loved it.
Person2: It wes a dream...!
Person1: Just like your mother - and all the others that felt him - and used his power - and went to hell with a smile!
Person2: Stop it!
Person1: Witches. Rowan.  that is what we are.
Person2: ENOUGH!!!

Person1: Andy?... What? What is it?  Andy... are you okay?
Person2: It was you... That night in the tunnels. You. You did this to me.
Person1: No I.... Andy, you can't be sure.
Person2: That's the scar where I stabbed you! Oh God... You deliberately took me down there so you could... God, I can't believe it!

Person1: Your girl's a menace.  Here, givver this... Getter off the street and teach her some humility.
Person2: I don't think so...
Person1: Well, then, you have insufficient information.  I'm grateful for this opportunity to set you straight.

Person1: No, of course not... I never killed anyone...
Person2: Then perhaps the temptation to kill would have been stronger... too strong perhaps...?
Person1: No!  I warned the English to go back home -- I begged them not to force us to fight -- they knew the defeat I would bring on them... why didn't they listen to me?
Person2: We have numerous witnesses who can confirm that you were not always carrying your banner...
Person1: Yes, probably... maybe...
Person2: So sometimes you were carrying just your sword?
Person1: Yes, but...
Person2: Did you use the sword that you held in your hand?
Person1: No, I... I held it up to...
Person2: You held up your sword and flourished it about in the air? Like this?
Person1: Yes, maybe... I don't remember...
Person2: ... so you were in the middle of the battlefield, with your sword in your hand, waving it above your head... charging against the enemy, screaming and yelling... fighting for your life... and you want us to believe that in the middle of all this excitement you never killed anyone?
Person1: No, I... I never killed anyone!

Person1: What else?
Person2: What else...? Let's see... You have complete freedom.
Person1: To do what?
Person2: I don't know... To grow, to go out. Whatever you want.
Person1: Anything?
Person2: Anything.
Person1: Like if I meet a handsome young man and I wanted to ask him to dance? I can do that?
Person2: Uh, if the guy wants to.
Person1: You don't think the guy would find me attractive enough to dance with?
Person2: Yes. I mean, no. I mean, maybe he would find her, I mean you attractive. Maybe he doesn't like to dance. Maybe all he likes to do is just stand around and drink and smoke and look cool with his buddies who don't dance either...
Person1: Maybe it doesn't matter if he's a good dancer cause it's a slow song, if that's what he's afraid of.
Person2: No... Maybe that's not the case. Maybe she shouldn't be such a smug little shit because she'd be surprised at what a good dancer he really is, but it's been a long time and he doesn't know if he's ready to...
Person1: Mike...

Person1: Don't help me.
Person2: He's a liar and a cheat and a scoundrel.  He's taken money from little old ladies.  He's talked people into doing things they never would have dreamed of.  Especially me.  But who has he really hurt?

Person1: We have to have a plan.
Person2: What sort of plan?
Person1: Our children cannot see us die first. If the Militia comes, you must hurry up to the roof. I will meet you there.
Person2: Please do not talk like this.
Person1: We have to. If I do not come, you must take them all by the hands and jump.

Person1: Please not be touching me, Conor.
Person2: I'll not take that kind of talk from you.  From those others below, maybe.  But not from you.
Person1: Leave me alone, Conor.  Please.
Person2: You're not talking sense, Mara!

Person1: It's fun!  It's high.
Person2: Or the sexual habits of locusts?
Person1: That was very interesting research George was doing!
Person2: What kind of Dead Head gets a temporary tattoo?
Person1: I already explained about that.
Person2: And where you ever really going to run the leper colony in Molokai?
Person1: Brian told you that?
Person2: Or maybe you just wanted to wear the headdress.
Person1: Every one of those times I was being supportive.  Something you won't understand.
Person2: Supportive?  You weren't being supportive.  You were being scared. Just like now.  You are the most lost woman I have ever laid eyes on.
Person1: Lost!
Person2: That's right.  You're so lost you don't even know how you like your eggs.
Person1: What!?
Person2: With the priest, you liked them scrambled.  With the Dead Head, fried. With the bug guy, poached.  Now it's egg whites only, thank you very much.
Person1: That's called changing your mind.
Person2: No, that's called not having a mind of your own.  What are you doing, Maggie? You really want to let that man drag you up Annapuma on your honeymoon?  You don't want to climb Annapuma.
Person1: Yes I do!
Person2: No you don't.  You want a man who will lead you down the beach with his head over your eyes just so you can discover the feel of the sand under your feet. You want a guy who will take you into a cave with a thousand candles just to read you a poem.  You want a man to wake you up at dawn because he's burning to talk to you and he can't wait another minute to find out what you'll say.  Am I right?

Person1: Now I'm the one who's nauseous. So what's the deal? What do you need me for?
Person2: There's some folks I want to keep tabs on. Dirty, tattooed, uncivilized. Your kind of people.
Person1: What do I get out of it?
Person2: If you find out what I want to know, and if I'm able to successfully use that information, you get to go back to your degenerate little life. If not, you take a bath.
Person1: You're one sick bastard. A sadist with a badge looking to rope me in to a suicide mission. I think no matter which way I go I'm likely to wind up face down on a sheet of plastic. So here's my answer

Person1: You don't remember me, Mr. Szpilman?
Person2: No, I don't think so?
Person1: Warsaw Radio. I was a technician. I saw you almost every day.
Person2: Sorry, I don't remember.
Person1: Doesn't matter. You've nothing to worry about. I'll visit often.

Person1: You know what we do to welchers, Cluett, don't you?
Person2: I know, I know, Dude. Oh, I must have been crazy! I lost my head completely!
Person1: That's your funeral. We've got fifty thousand dollars comin' to us.
Person2: I haven't got it.

Person1: He was a town marshal... one night he surprised two burglars, coming out the back of a drugstore... They shot him.
Person2: Killed outright?
Person1: No. He was strong, he lasted almost a month. My mother - died when I was very young, so my father had become - the whole world to me... After he left me, I had nobody. I was ten years old.
Person2: You're very frank, Clarice. I think - it would be quite something to know you in private life.
Person1: Quid pro quo, Doctor.
Person2: The significance of the moth is change. Caterpillar into cocoon into beauty... Billy wants to change, too, Clarice. But there's the problem of his size, you see. Even if he were a woman, he'd have to be a big one...
Person1: Dr. Lecter, there's no correlation in the literature between transsexualism and violence. Transsexuals are very passive.
Person2: Clever girl. You're so close to the way you're going to catch him - do you realize that?
Person1: No. Tell me why.
Person2: After your father's death, you were orphaned. What happened next?  I don't imagine the answer's on those second-rate shoes, Clarice.
Person1: I went to live with my mother's cousin and her husband in Montana. They had a ranch.
Person2: A cattle ranch?
Person1: Horses - and sheep...
Person2: How long did you live there?
Person1: Two months.
Person2: Why so briefly?
Person1: I - ran away...
Person2: Why, Clarice? Did the rancher harm you?
Person1: No.
Person2: Did he try to?
Person1: No...! Quid pro quo, Doctor.
Person2: Billy's not a real transsexual, but he thinks he is. He tries to be. He's tried to be a lot of things, I expect.
Person1: You said - I was very close to the way we'd catch him.
Person2: There are three major centers for transsexual surgery: Johns Hopkins, the University of Minnesota, and Columbus Medical center. I wouldn't be surprised if Billy has applied for sex reassignment at one or all of them, and been rejected.
Person1: On what basis would they reject him?
Person2: The personality inventories would trip him up. Rorschach, Wechsler, House-Tree-Person... He wouldn't test like a real transsexual.
Person1: How would he test?

Person1: The Matrix is everywhere, it's all around us, here even in this room. You can see it out your window or on your television.  You feel it when you go to work, or go to church or pay your taxes.  It is the world that has been pulled over your eyes to blind you from the truth.
Person2: What truth?
Person1: That you are a slave, Neo.  Like everyone else, you were born into bondage, kept inside a prison that you cannot smell, taste, or touch. A prison for your mind.

Person1: Get with it.  Get over it. ...millions of galaxies of hundreds of millions of stars and a speck on one in a blink...that's us.  Lost in space.  The universe doesn't care.  The cop, you, me?  Who notices?
Person2: What happened to you?
Person1: As in...?
Person2: Man, if someone had a gun to your head and said: "You gotta tell me what's goin' on with that person over there or I'll kill you"...they'd have to kill you...  'Cause you don't have a clue for...or about...anyone...  To be like that, I don't think you, you have any of that for your own life...  Do you believe you're entitled or at least expect to draw breath in the a.m.?  Open your eyes in the morning?  I don't think you do...I don't think so...  I think you are way low...like in your estimation.  In your estimation of yourself.  So how'd you get that way...?
Person1: ...all the cabbies in LA, I pull Max, the man with X-ray vision...
Person2: Answer the question.
Person1: Look in the mirror.  ...piss-ant paper towels...a bottle of 409...saving up for goin' to the Comoros.  How much you got saved?
Person2: None of your business.
Person1: ...pie in the sky?  "Someday my dream'll come..."  But one night you'll wake up and realize suddenly you're old.  It hasn't happened.  It never will.  Life just flipped on you.  Tomorrow became yesterday.  Then you'll bullshit yourself it was never gonna happen, anyway, and push it back in memory...and anesthetize yourself in a Barcalounger with daytime TV for the rest of your life...  Don't talk to me about murder.  You're do-in' yourself...in this yellow prison with steel-belted radials. Clocking in and out everyday...

Person1: You're better when you take them.
Person2: I know, I know.  But still, somehow I don't feel better.
Person1: All right.  How about I feel better when you take them?

Person1: Come on, Fran -- don't be like that. You just going to sit there and keep bawling?  You won't talk to me, you won't tell me what's wrong --  Look, I know you think I'm stalling you. But when you've been married to a woman for twelve years, you don't just sit down at the breakfast table and say "Pass the sugar -- and I want a divorce." It's not that easy.  Anyway, this is the wrong time. The kids are home from school -- my in- laws are visiting for the holidays -- I can't bring it up now.  This isn't like you, Fran -- you were always such a good sport -- such fun to be with --
Person2: Yeah -- that's me. The Happy Idiot -- a million laughs.
Person1: Well, that's more like it. At least you're speaking to me.
Person2: Funny thing happened to me at the office party today -- I ran into your secretary -- Miss Olsen. You know -- ring-a-ding-ding? I laughed so much I like to died.
Person1: Is that what's been bothering you -- Miss Olsen? That's ancient history.
Person2: I was never very good at history. Let me see -- there was Miss Olsen, and then there was Miss Rossi -- no, she came before -- it was Miss Koch who came after Miss Olsen --
Person1: Now, Fran --
Person2: And just think -- right now there's some lucky girl in the building who's going to come after me --
Person1: Okay, okay, Fran. I deserve that. But just ask yourself -- why does a man run around with a lot of girls? Because he's unhappy at home -- because he's lonely, that's why -- all that was before you, Fran -- I've stopped running.

Person1: It's hormonal, Nathan. I can't help it. I'm sorry.
Person2: Your entire body?
Person1: I'm getting electrolysis. It's working, but it takes time. So meanwhile I have to...
Person2: You have to shave? Like an ape?
Person1: Apes don't shave, you son of a bitch!
Person2: Don't quibble. You know what I mean.
Person1: I'm sorry. Please don't be mad at me for this.
Person2: Mad? I'm I'm... disgusted!
Person1: I'm the same person I was before you knew, damn it! Oh God!
Person2: I have to think! I have to think!

Person1: Ooooooh-kayyyyyy.
Person2: In the end, the jury saw it my way.
Person1: Well, then it all seems to be working out for you.  Listen, Ms...?
Person2: Do you remember Henry Birdson?  The math professor who won the Salwen Medal, then went home and killed his family?
Person1: What's that got to do with you?
Person2: Last week the Catholic Church certified him as genuinely possessed.  You realize how rarely they make that ruling?
Person1: I do.
Person2: An exorcism was just held for Birdson. It failed.  Disastrously.  Which means there's still a demonic spirit present in his body.  I was wondering, perhaps you'd like to go meet Henry.  He's pretty amazing.
Person1: I'll bet.
Person2: On television, you seemed so sure of yourself.  If you're really that confident, you'd be willing to let someone prove you wrong?
Person1: The world would be so much simpler if it were all just about good and evil. Unfortunately I find it much more slippery and elusive place.
Person2: Well, I think it's very simple.  Here's a tape of Birdson's exorcism.  I'll be at the Public Library.  Tomorrow at 2... if you want to meet him.

Person1: Don't sneak up on me...
Person2: Uh, it's just--that woman out there-- that horrible Hag. She's the one who keeps following me on her creepy little scooter--And now she's built a hut in the back..Why did you...
Person1: Because she asked me--and I couldn't very well turn her down. Don't you remember-- of course you don't remember--that "Hag" is the one who brought you to that hospital in Gotham City. For what it's worth-- currently not much--we owe her your life...When I think about a single woman in Gotham City--amnesia is probably the best thing that could happen to a girl like you...Oh, don't forget your visor.

Person1: Now, if you'll excuse me, I really must...
Person2: Wait. Stop. You don't seem to understand. You're not really comprehending any of this. I killed him. I did it, Carnes. I'm Patrick Bateman. I chopped Owen's head off. I tortured dozens of girls. The whole message I left on your machine was true.
Person1: Excuse me. I really must he going.
Person2: No! Listen, don't you know who I am? I'm not Davis, I'm Patrick Bateman! I talk to you on the phone all the time! Don't you recognize me? You're my lawyer.

Person1: Yes...
Person2: You want me to wear white for you? You must be madder than I thought...
Person1: But ELLEN, I told you I have stopped...
Person2: But for how long - what will set you off again? You'll be walking down the street and some girl will be wearing a white hat - and that will be it - back on the merry go round...

Person1: Where'd you say the showersand thingswere?
Person2: Heyyou're little, aren't you?
Person1: Where is the shower?
Person2: Your hair's cute like that. You should never comb it.
Person1: I'll find it myself.

Person1: But maybe with a good start I can hit those hand-holds.
Person2: Hand-holds?!  I can barely see them.
Person1: We don't have time to argue about it!
Person2: Are you crazy?  Has the altitude shrunk your brain, Gabe?
Person1: Take the rope.
Person2: I won't do it.  No way.
Person1: Take the rope.
Person2: Enough's enough.  How could anybody in their right mind... then again, you never were in your right mind.
Person1: Wrap it around that rock twice.
Person2: I'm going to wrap it around your throat!
Person1: An' if I miss, dig in and try your best to slow the fall.
Person2: Forget it!  I refuse!
Person1: Fine, it shouldn't bother your conscience.
Person2: Don't lay any guilt on me. Suicide's a personal thing, best done alone.

Person1: What happened to you?
Person2: Dad, did you do it?  Did you take that money?
Person1: Sweetheart, no.  Don't be ridiculous.
Person2: Swear to god, I need to know the truth.
Person1: Honey!
Person2: Swear to god.
Person1: I swear to god.
Person2: But I found the money.
Person1: Honey, it's not what you think.
Person2: What am I supposed to think?
Person1: Honey, about what?
Person2: You stole from them, you lied to me.  How do you think that's supposed to make me feel?
Person1: You think you know what this money is?
Person2: Yes, I...
Person1: No, you don't know what it is.  It's not for me.  This money's for you, for when you come back from England with honors to set you up, so you don't have to depend on anybody again.  Diane, I take better care of these people than their families do.  I care for them, I give them flowers, I feed them, I wipe their mouths...
Person2: No, but you stole from them.
Person1: I made their lives better.
Person2: You let me believe you.  You knew you were guilty, and you let me become a part of it.  God, how... how could you...
Person1: Alright, alright.  Go ahead.  When I'm old, give me someone like me, but go ahead.
Person2: I trusted you.  I stood up in court five years ago and I picked you.
Person1: Why are you being so hateful to me?  Is this because of Lloyd?
Person2: No.  I told you everything, you lied to me.  I would have done anything for you.
Person1: That's right, work it out.
Person2: No, I will.  I don't want to leave something out because I know I can say anything to you.  You're a liar, and a thief.
Person1: Take it easy how mad you make me, I'm the only dad you've got.
Person2: I'm so ashamed dad.
Person1: Don't say that, it's not good for you.  You're...
Person2: You're right.

Person1: That's right. That's what I said. You have a good memory. Then what?
Person2: Well.
Person1: THEN WHAT?
Person2: Then you got undressed.
Person1: How many times have you sneaked into girls' apartments and watched them undress?
Person2: Never before this.
Person1: How'd you like it if someone sneaked into your house and watched you.  Get undressed. I want to see you.
Person2: No. Come on.
Person1: NO, you come on. Take off your pants. I want to see you.
Person2: Look. I'm sorry. Just let me leave.
Person1: No way.

Person1: A name. Grunemann. Nothing. And they showed me pictures like this and they meant nothing. Then they asked me, well had I been getting letters, from someone out there in Cabbageville --
Person2: -- Tuscarora --
Person1: All right, yes, I had been. Those sick, wild letters -- I'm watching you, gonna follow you, gonna punish you, kill you et cetera. Well, they said, all right that's Grunemann. So try to remember when you and he - when -- well I don't know, there was that dumper once, he sounded like that dumper --  Dumpers; they get their kicks beating you up. A man hired me once, then tried to really kill me - that'd be about two years ago.

Person1: You had me worried to death!
Person2: But Sean said...
Person1: I'm the one you should be listening to! Do you think dropping an anchor in the middle of a storm makes any sense whatsoever?

Person1: You got me something?  What's the occasion?
Person2: You deserve it is the occasion.

Person1: Listen to me, Neo.  I know why you're afraid to go through that door.  You can't get free of thinking that I could be wrong.  You must believe me.  There is a greatness inside of you, Neo.  A greatness that is going to lift you to unimaginable heights and that in time will change the world.
Person2: Morpheus, I don't know --
Person1: I know.  That is why it is so important for you to go.  Please, Neo, I'm asking you to find whatever respect you may have for me and trust me.

Person1: All I know is that you are not doing yourself or the baby any good by acting like a crazy woman.  Why don't you take off - leave Springwood and cool out somewhere for a while?
Person2: Goddamn it, Yvonne!  You don't just run away from this guy!  He finds you in your dreams.

Person1: I watched her grow up. Just like you. I know how her mind worked. She kept snooping around because she was worried about you. What you'd gotten yourself into. So stop blaming me. And blame yourself.
Person2: I do. Every day.
Person1: Erin knows, doesn't she?

Person1: Come on.
Person2: What's the matter?
Person1: What do you mean, 'What's the matter?' I made a lot of money for you. I want my cut.
Person2: What money? I've seen you stealing from me.
Person1: What money?  Look at this stack of chips. Don't give me that shit. I want my end.
Person2: Ginger, I've been watching you all night. You've been stealing from me.
Person1: Don't give me that shit. I want my money.
Person2: That bag's full of chips you stole.
Person1: What do you mean 'stole'? I didn't steal anything from you.

Person1: Hello.
Person2: It's me.
Person1: I figured it was.  Where are you?
Person2: I think the big question here is where are you, if you don't mind my saying so, and I think I know where you are.  You're running.  On the run.  You're running from a point that everyone hits in any relationship, and you're just going to hit it again with Ian but it's going to be with a World Music bunny- rabbit-looking earth-shoe-wearing "Doctor Who"-watching twit who doesn't really understand you, not the way that I do and will more in the future, and you'll have just wasted more time and arrive in the exact same place that you're in now, only later. And with... him.
Person1: I'm not -- hold on...

Person1: I've been hearing rumors that you're thinking of joining our competitors.
Person2: It's just an offer I'm considering. Nothing's decided yet.
Person1: After all we've been through? All the projects we've worked on together?
Person2: It's not personal, Mason. It's about what's best for my career.
Person1: I’ve always been there for you; you can’t doubt me now. Think about the team and what we've built.

Person1: My dear Aulon, to you it shall befall the honor of impersonating the royal blood!
Person2: Majesty, I can't...
Person1: Why, are you not ready to die for your king?
Person2: Of course I am, it's just that...
Person1: Good.  Until then, feel free to live like a king!

Person1: You can't do it alone, ya know.  It takes a mob of guys like you and enough money to make 'em look good.
Person2: We'll get by without 'em.
Person1: This isn't like playin' winos on the street.  You gotta do more than outrun the guy.
Person2: I never played for winos.
Person1: You gotta keep Lonnegan's con, even after you spent his money.  And no matter how much you take from his, he'll get more.
Person2: You're sacred of 'em, aren't ya?
Person1: Right down to my socks, turkey.  If I'da been half as scared a that lop-ear, I wouldn't a fallen asleep on 'em.  Lonnegan might kill me, but at least he won't bore me to death.
Person2: Then you'll do it?
Person1: If I can find a mob that'll risk it. But no matter what happens, I don't want you comin' back to me halfway through and sayin' it's not enough... cause it's all you got.

Person1: I'm thinking about getting out of town this week.  You want to come with me?
Person2: Where are you going?
Person1: I don't know.  Maybe California.
Person2: You swear?

Person1: We went straight to Houston, and then Saturday night we drove to Galveston and stayed over there.
